metrics.name: go_gc_duration_seconds
metrics.help: A summary of the wall-time pause (stop-the-world) duration in garbage collection cycles.

metrics.name: go_gc_gogc_percent
metrics.help: Heap size target percentage configured by the user, otherwise 100. This value is set by the GOGC environment variable, and the runtime/debug.SetGCPercent function. Sourced from /gc/gogc:percent

metrics.name: go_gc_gomemlimit_bytes
metrics.help: Go runtime memory limit configured by the user, otherwise math.MaxInt64. This value is set by the GOMEMLIMIT environment variable, and the runtime/debug.SetMemoryLimit function. Sourced from /gc/gomemlimit:bytes

metrics.name: go_goroutines
metrics.help: Number of goroutines that currently exist.

metrics.name: go_info
metrics.help: Information about the Go environment.

metrics.name: go_memstats_alloc_bytes
metrics.help: Number of bytes allocated in heap and currently in use. Equals to /memory/classes/heap/objects:bytes.

metrics.name: go_memstats_alloc_bytes_total
metrics.help: Total number of bytes allocated in heap until now, even if released already. Equals to /gc/heap/allocs:bytes.

metrics.name: go_memstats_buck_hash_sys_bytes
metrics.help: Number of bytes used by the profiling bucket hash table. Equals to /memory/classes/profiling/buckets:bytes.

metrics.name: go_memstats_frees_total
metrics.help: Total number of heap objects frees. Equals to /gc/heap/frees:objects + /gc/heap/tiny/allocs:objects.

metrics.name: go_memstats_gc_sys_bytes
metrics.help: Number of bytes used for garbage collection system metadata. Equals to /memory/classes/metadata/other:bytes.

metrics.name: go_memstats_heap_alloc_bytes
metrics.help: Number of heap bytes allocated and currently in use, same as go_memstats_alloc_bytes. Equals to /memory/classes/heap/objects:bytes.

metrics.name: go_memstats_heap_idle_bytes
metrics.help: Number of heap bytes waiting to be used. Equals to /memory/classes/heap/released:bytes + /memory/classes/heap/free:bytes.

metrics.name: go_memstats_heap_inuse_bytes
metrics.help: Number of heap bytes that are in use. Equals to /memory/classes/heap/objects:bytes + /memory/classes/heap/unused:bytes

metrics.name: go_memstats_heap_objects
metrics.help: Number of currently allocated objects. Equals to /gc/heap/objects:objects.

metrics.name: go_memstats_heap_released_bytes
metrics.help: Number of heap bytes released to OS. Equals to /memory/classes/heap/released:bytes.

metrics.name: go_memstats_heap_sys_bytes
metrics.help: Number of heap bytes obtained from system. Equals to /memory/classes/heap/objects:bytes + /memory/classes/heap/unused:bytes + /memory/classes/heap/released:bytes + /memory/classes/heap/free:bytes.

metrics.name: go_memstats_last_gc_time_seconds
metrics.help: Number of seconds since 1970 of last garbage collection.

metrics.name: go_memstats_mallocs_total
metrics.help: Total number of heap objects allocated, both live and gc-ed. Semantically a counter version for go_memstats_heap_objects gauge. Equals to /gc/heap/allocs:objects + /gc/heap/tiny/allocs:objects.

metrics.name: go_memstats_mcache_inuse_bytes
metrics.help: Number of bytes in use by mcache structures. Equals to /memory/classes/metadata/mcache/inuse:bytes.

metrics.name: go_memstats_mcache_sys_bytes

metrics.help: Count of completed GC cycles generated by the Go runtime.

metrics.help: Count of completed GC cycles forced by the application.

metrics.name: go_gc_cycles_total_gc_cycles_total
metrics.help: Count of all completed GC cycles.

metrics.help: A summary of the pause duration of garbage collection cycles.

metrics.name: go_gc_heap_allocs_by_size_bytes_total_bucket
metrics.help: Distribution of heap allocations by approximate size. Note that this does not include tiny objects as defined by /gc/heap/tiny/allocs:objects, only tiny blocks.

metrics.name: go_gc_heap_allocs_bytes_total
metrics.help: Cumulative sum of memory allocated to the heap by the application.

metrics.name: go_gc_heap_allocs_objects_total
metrics.help: Cumulative count of heap allocations triggered by the application. Note that this does not include tiny objects as defined by /gc/heap/tiny/allocs:objects, only tiny blocks.

metrics.name: go_gc_heap_frees_by_size_bytes_total_bucket
metrics.help: Distribution of freed heap allocations by approximate size. Note that this does not include tiny objects as defined by /gc/heap/tiny/allocs:objects, only tiny blocks.

metrics.name: go_gc_heap_frees_bytes_total
metrics.help: Cumulative sum of heap memory freed by the garbage collector.

metrics.name: go_gc_heap_frees_objects_total
metrics.help: Cumulative count of heap allocations whose storage was freed by the garbage collector. Note that this does not include tiny objects as defined by /gc/heap/tiny/allocs:objects, only tiny blocks.

metrics.name: go_gc_heap_goal_bytes
metrics.help: Heap size target for the end of the GC cycle.

metrics.name: go_gc_heap_objects_objects
metrics.help: Number of objects, live or unswept, occupying heap memory.

metrics.name: go_gc_heap_tiny_allocs_objects_total
metrics.help: Count of small allocations that are packed together into blocks. These allocations are counted separately from other allocations because each individual allocation is not tracked by the runtime, only their block. Each block is already accounted for in allocs-by-size and frees-by-size.

metrics.name: go_gc_pauses_seconds_total_bucket
metrics.help: Distribution individual GC-related stop-the-world pause latencies.

metrics.name: go_goroutines
metrics.help: Number of goroutines that currently exist.

metrics.name: go_info
metrics.help: Information about the Go environment.

metrics.name: go_memory_classes_heap_free_bytes
metrics.help: Memory that is completely free and eligible to be returned to the underlying system, but has not been. This metric is the runtime's estimate of free address space that is backed by physical memory.

metrics.name: go_memory_classes_heap_objects_bytes
metrics.help: Memory occupied by live objects and dead objects that have not yet been marked free by the garbage collector.

metrics.name: go_memory_classes_heap_released_bytes
metrics.help: Memory that is completely free and has been returned to the underlying system. This metric is the runtime's estimate of free address space that is still mapped into the process, but is not backed by physical memory.

metrics.name: go_memory_classes_heap_stacks_bytes
metrics.help: Memory allocated from the heap that is reserved for stack space, whether or not it is currently in-use.

metrics.name: go_memory_classes_heap_unused_bytes
metrics.help: Memory that is reserved for heap objects but is not currently used to hold heap objects.

metrics.name: go_memory_classes_metadata_mcache_free_bytes
metrics.help: Memory that is reserved for runtime mcache structures, but not in-use.

metrics.name: go_memory_classes_metadata_mcache_inuse_bytes
metrics.help: Memory that is occupied by runtime mcache structures that are currently being used.

metrics.name: go_memory_classes_metadata_mspan_free_bytes
metrics.help: Memory that is reserved for runtime mspan structures, but not in-use.

metrics.name: go_memory_classes_metadata_mspan_inuse_bytes
metrics.help: Memory that is occupied by runtime mspan structures that are currently being used.

metrics.name: go_memory_classes_metadata_other_bytes
metrics.help: Memory that is reserved for or used to hold runtime metadata.

metrics.name: go_memory_classes_os_stacks_bytes
metrics.help: Stack memory allocated by the underlying operating system.

metrics.name: go_memory_classes_other_bytes
metrics.help: Memory used by execution trace buffers, structures for debugging the runtime, finalizer and profiler specials, and more.

metrics.name: go_memory_classes_profiling_buckets_bytes
metrics.help: Memory that is used by the stack trace hash map used for profiling.

metrics.name: go_memory_classes_total_bytes
metrics.help: All memory mapped by the Go runtime into the current process as read-write. Note that this does not include memory mapped by code called via cgo or via the syscall package. Sum of all metrics in /memory/classes.

metrics.help: Number of tuple inserts, updates, or deletes prior to analyze as a fraction of reltuples.

metrics.help: Minimum number of tuple inserts, updates, or deletes prior to analyze.

metrics.help: Time to sleep between autovacuum runs. [Units converted to seconds.]

metrics.help: Vacuum cost delay in milliseconds, for autovacuum. [Units converted to seconds.]

metrics.name: pg_settings_autovacuum_vacuum_cost_limit
metrics.help: Vacuum cost amount available before napping, for autovacuum.

metrics.name: pg_settings_autovacuum_vacuum_insert_scale_factor
metrics.help: Number of tuple inserts prior to vacuum as a fraction of reltuples.

metrics.name: pg_settings_autovacuum_vacuum_insert_threshold
metrics.help: Minimum number of tuple inserts prior to vacuum, or -1 to disable insert vacuums.

metrics.name: pg_settings_autovacuum_vacuum_scale_factor
metrics.help: Number of tuple updates or deletes prior to vacuum as a fraction of reltuples.

metrics.name: pg_settings_autovacuum_vacuum_threshold
metrics.help: Minimum number of tuple updates or deletes prior to vacuum.

metrics.name: pg_settings_autovacuum_work_mem_bytes

metrics.name: pg_settings_enable_tidscan
metrics.help: Enables the planner's use of TID scan plans.

metrics.name: pg_settings_escape_string_warning

metrics.name: pg_settings_fsync

metrics.name: pg_settings_hash_mem_multiplier

metrics.name: pg_settings_idle_in_transaction_session_timeout_seconds

metrics.name: go_gc_duration_seconds
metrics.help: A summary of the wall-time pause (stop-the-world) duration in garbage collection cycles.

metrics.name: go_gc_gogc_percent
metrics.help: Heap size target percentage configured by the user, otherwise 100. This value is set by the GOGC environment variable, and the runtime/debug.SetGCPercent function. Sourced from /gc/gogc:percent

metrics.name: go_gc_gomemlimit_bytes
metrics.help: Go runtime memory limit configured by the user, otherwise math.MaxInt64. This value is set by the GOMEMLIMIT environment variable, and the runtime/debug.SetMemoryLimit function. Sourced from /gc/gomemlimit:bytes

metrics.name: go_goroutines
metrics.help: Number of goroutines that currently exist.

metrics.name: go_info
metrics.help: Information about the Go environment.

metrics.name: go_memstats_alloc_bytes
metrics.help: Number of bytes allocated in heap and currently in use. Equals to /memory/classes/heap/objects:bytes.

metrics.name: go_memstats_alloc_bytes_total
metrics.help: Total number of bytes allocated in heap until now, even if released already. Equals to /gc/heap/allocs:bytes.

metrics.name: go_memstats_buck_hash_sys_bytes
metrics.help: Number of bytes used by the profiling bucket hash table. Equals to /memory/classes/profiling/buckets:bytes.

metrics.name: go_memstats_frees_total
metrics.help: Total number of heap objects frees. Equals to /gc/heap/frees:objects + /gc/heap/tiny/allocs:objects.

metrics.name: go_memstats_gc_sys_bytes
metrics.help: Number of bytes used for garbage collection system metadata. Equals to /memory/classes/metadata/other:bytes.

metrics.name: go_memstats_heap_alloc_bytes
metrics.help: Number of heap bytes allocated and currently in use, same as go_memstats_alloc_bytes. Equals to /memory/classes/heap/objects:bytes.

metrics.name: go_memstats_heap_idle_bytes
metrics.help: Number of heap bytes waiting to be used. Equals to /memory/classes/heap/released:bytes + /memory/classes/heap/free:bytes.

metrics.name: go_memstats_heap_inuse_bytes
metrics.help: Number of heap bytes that are in use. Equals to /memory/classes/heap/objects:bytes + /memory/classes/heap/unused:bytes

metrics.name: go_memstats_heap_objects
metrics.help: Number of currently allocated objects. Equals to /gc/heap/objects:objects.

metrics.name: go_memstats_heap_released_bytes
metrics.help: Number of heap bytes released to OS. Equals to /memory/classes/heap/released:bytes.

metrics.name: go_memstats_heap_sys_bytes
metrics.help: Number of heap bytes obtained from system. Equals to /memory/classes/heap/objects:bytes + /memory/classes/heap/unused:bytes + /memory/classes/heap/released:bytes + /memory/classes/heap/free:bytes.

metrics.name: go_memstats_last_gc_time_seconds
metrics.help: Number of seconds since 1970 of last garbage collection.

metrics.name: go_memstats_mallocs_total
metrics.help: Total number of heap objects allocated, both live and gc-ed. Semantically a counter version for go_memstats_heap_objects gauge. Equals to /gc/heap/allocs:objects + /gc/heap/tiny/allocs:objects.

metrics.name: go_memstats_mcache_inuse_bytes

metrics.name: go_memstats_sys_bytes

metrics.name: stackdriver_cloudsql_database_cloudsql_googleapis_com_database_instance_state
metrics.help:
The current serving state of the Cloud SQL instance.
Because there are seven possible states, seven data points are returned.
Each of them has a different field value representing each state.
Only the one that matches the current state of the instance is TRUE. All the others are FALSE.
The state can be one of the following:
RUNNING:  The instance is running, or is ready to run when accessed.
SUSPENDED: The instance is not available, for example due to problems with billing.
RUNNABLE: The instance has been stopped by owner. It is not currently running, but it's ready to be restarted.
PENDING_CREATE: The instance is being created.
MAINTENANCE: The instance is down for maintenance.
FAILED: The instance creation failed.
UNKNOWN_STATE: The state of the instance is unknown.

The lazy writer is a system process that flushes out batches of dirty, aged buffers (buffers that contain changes that must be written back to disk before the buffer can be reused for a different page) and makes them available to user processes.

metrics.help: Current number of pending I/Os that are waiting to be completed. Each scheduler has a list of pending I/Os that are checked to determine whether they have been completed every time there is a context switch. The count is incremented when the request is inserted. This count is decremented when the request is completed. This number does not indicate the state of the I/Os.

rules.description: {{ $labels.instance }} of job {{ $labels.job }} has been down for more than 2 minutes.
{{ $labels.instance }} of cluster {{ $labels.cluster_name }} has been down for more than 2 minutes.
AS can't write any bin on {{ $labels.ns }} @ {{ $labels.cluster_name }}, please dig ASAP
AS can't access to all partitions for {{ $labels.ns }} @ {{ $labels.cluster_name }}
AS Low Freespace for {{ $labels.ns }} @ {{ $labels.instance }}
AS Low Memory for {{ $labels.ns }} @ {{ $labels.instance }}, if it continues, cluster will be down for OOM, please dig

rules.summary: AS - Stop Write Limit close for {{ $labels.ns }} @ {{ $labels.instance }} (< 15%)
rules.description: AS is configure to stop write when threshold is {{ printf "aerospike_namespace_stop_writes_pct{instance="%s",ns="%s"}" .labels.instance .labels.ns }}, it remains only 15% before reaching this limit

rules.summary: AS - Soon Stop Write because of close for {{ $labels.ns }} @ {{ $labels.instance }} (< 15%)
rules.description: Ensure clocks are synchronized across the cluster. {{ $labels.ns }} @ {{ $labels.instance }} (< 15%)

rules.summary: AS - Soon Stop Write because of close for {{ $labels.ns }} @ {{ $labels.instance }} (> 20s)
rules.description: Ensure clocks are synchronized across the cluster. {{ $labels.ns }} @ {{ $labels.instance }} (> 20s)

rules.summary: AS - Stop Write because of close for {{ $labels.ns }} @ {{ $labels.instance }} (> 40s)
rules.description: Ensure clocks are synchronized across the cluster. {{ $labels.ns }} @ {{ $labels.instance }} (> 40s)
Heap efficiency is very low for {{ $labels.instance }} (< 50%). You should check your configuration
Backup for {{ $labels.instance }}/{{ $labels.namespace }} has fail
Backup for {{ $labels.pop }}/{{ $labels.namespace }} has fail

rules.expr:
(
  node_memory_MemAvailable_bytes or
  (
    node_memory_Buffers_bytes +
    node_memory_Cached_bytes +
    node_memory_MemFree_bytes +
    node_memory_Slab_bytes
  )
) / node_memory_MemTotal_bytes

rules.description: node_exporter seems down on {{$labels.instance}}, then the host is probably down
Temperature has exceeded 80° for more than 30 min on {{ $labels.instance }}. Please open a ticket on OVH/LW/whatever
CPU has been throttled on {{ $labels.instance }} for at least 5 minuts, it directly impact performances because it reduces frequency. Please check T° of your processor
Slow CPU on {{ $labels.instance }} ({{ $value }}) for at least 5 minuts (< 30% of maximum frequency). It directly impact performances because it reduces frequency.
Cant scrape {{ $labels.instance }} for more than 5 minuts, please check the instance
Check why `{{ $labels.file}}` hasn't been scraped on {{ $labels.instance}} for 2 minuts
Check why `{{ $labels.file }}` hasn't been scraped on {{ $labels.instance}} for 2 minuts
Check why `{{ $labels.file }}` hasn't been scraped on {{ $labels.instance}} for 2 minuts
High drop detected (> 10%) on {{ $labels.device }} @ {{ $labels.instance }}. Misconfiguration or NIC has an issue

rules.expr:
haproxy:backend_http_responses_5xx:rate5m
/ on(namespace, proxy) group_left()
clamp_min(
  sum by (namespace, proxy) (haproxy:backend_http_responses:rate5m),
  1
) * 100

(haproxy:backend_http_responses_4xx:rate5m > 10)
/ on(namespace, proxy) group_left()
clamp_min(
  sum by (namespace, proxy) (haproxy:backend_http_responses:rate5m),
  1
) * 100

(
  (haproxy:backend_http_responses_4xx:rate5m > 10)
  + haproxy:backend_http_responses_5xx:rate5m
)
/ on(namespace, proxy) group_left()
clamp_min(
  sum by (namespace, proxy) (haproxy:backend_http_responses:rate5m),
  1
)
